column1: # Sentinel ↔ Claude Code boundary contract — machine-readable manifest.
# Consumed by scripts/cc_contract_check.sh, which greps each `expect` regex
# against a deobfuscated CC bundle and reports drift.
#   kind          env | field | method | event | enum | deprecation
#   must_exist    true  = pattern MUST be present (absence = sentinel breaks → FAIL)
#                 false = pattern must be ABSENT (presence = FAIL)
#                 warn  = presence is a WARNing (deprecation watch; still works today)
#   severity      high | med | low   (only affects reporting weight)
#   sentinel_ref  where sentinel depends on this (file:line-ish)
#   expect        egrep -o pattern to search for in the bundle
# Blank lines and lines starting with # are ignored.

column7: note
CC exports the session UUID as CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID into hook/MCP children; producer+dispatcher key on it.
CC exports the project directory path; used for [Project Context] injection + hookdeck scoping.
CC sets CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT (no underscore before POINT) as a caller-attestation marker.
CC exports the per-session config dir; used as a session-key fallback.
PostToolUse tool output is sent under the key tool_response (NOT tool_result); HookInput aliases it.
agent_type is a top-level field on hook payloads (SubagentStart/Stop); read typed, not from extra.
Pre/PostToolUse carry tool_input; many hooks extract the bash command from it.
Every hook payload carries session_id; all session-scoped state keys on it.
The MCP channel push wire method; sentinel-mcp delivers events over it.
SessionStart hookSpecificOutput.initialUserMessage drives the /reload-plugins autoheal.
The envelope key CC reads structured hook output from.
The field sentinel injects context through (UserPromptSubmit/SessionStart etc.).
Dual-display notice channel (production_override etc.).
CC's permissionDecision enum accepts defer (sentinel added it; keep in step).
